photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: black facade with bright pink, red, blue, and aqua mural swirls on the main roll-down door
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade appears freshly painted and branded, with clear signage and good overall upkeep despite lane-edge wear at the side walls.
photo_observations.window_details: No conventional windows visible on the observed frontage; the facade is dominated by a branded roll-down opening.
photo_observations.door_details: Primary frontage reads as a large roll-down commercial opening with signage above rather than a separate pedestrian door.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple fascia capped by a branded signboard and decorative mural panel at the parapet line.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Branded mural door, decorative parapet panel, and side blade sign create the visual interest.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Compact branded commercial frontage with a large painted roll-down opening facing the street and alley edge.
photo_observations.signage_observed: Segovia Foods, Segovia casa del chorizo, 218 Augusta Avenue, Segovia Spanish Market.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Frontage modernized with mural-painted roll-down door and contemporary signage treatment.
photo_observations.notes: Observed at the alley edge beside the storefront.

storefront_description: Compact branded commercial frontage with a large painted roll-down opening facing the street and alley edge.

facade_description: 218 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a black facade with bright pink, red, blue, and aqua mural swirls on the main roll-down door palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s
